review: PEAT learning-curriculum hardening to enterprise-onboarding grade
phases_completed: Phase 1 — Ground truth established (per-repo audits + merged ground-truth.md)
Phase 2 — Adversarial audit of the curriculum (14 per-document ledgers + merged claim-ledger.md)
Phase 5 — Use cases & examples (five worked, leg-by-leg labeled)
Phase 6 — Curriculum rewrite (hub + constrained track + 12 markdown modules, in place)

peat-node.head: 9fcdabd
peat-node.subject: fix(attachments): mirror outbox layout in peat-cli + test inbox sinks (#173) + docs/version-minimums (#175)
peat-node.origin_status: advanced bbe3b68->9fcdabd this run (v0.4.8: inbox mirrors sender outbox layout w/ path re-sanitisation #173, post-write blob_size validation, startup version banner, --print-config/PEAT_NODE_PRINT_CONFIG). proto/sidecar.proto unchanged — RPC count still 27.

open_todos: ADR-059 NUMBER COLLISION (peat#997, 2026-06-22): docs/adr/059-data-as-a-capability.md was added with the same number as the existing docs/adr/059-cross-transport-document-bridging.md. The curriculum's ADR-059 citations (Module 1 §1.5 'Amendment 4' cycle-break, Module 3 §3.5, diagram M-025) refer to the cross-transport-bridging ADR and remain correct; the new data-as-a-capability ADR (Proposed) is logged but deliberately NOT woven into prose to avoid conflating the two. Re-audit if the source repo renumbers one, or if data-as-a-capability gains code (then surface it where capability advertisement / ADR-018 is taught).

ADR-072 (Proposed, peat#996): synced-folder lifecycle & file-handling policy (deletion / idempotent re-drop / version ordering on the ADR-071 distribution document). No code. Re-audit Module 3 §3.4b / Module 8 when a lifecycle policy field lands on the distribution document and peat-node begins honoring it (flips from Proposed to Shipped).

ADR-071 Phase-1 follow-on: the convergence path is wired but inert — publish writes collection:None (file_distribution.rs ~963 TODO) and no NeedEvaluator is attached by default. Re-audit Module 3 §3.4b / Module 2 when the sidecar starts binding a collection on publish and registering durable subscriptions (the gRPC surface), which will flip interest-driven delivery from Proposed-seam to Shipped
Track ADR-071 Phases 2-3 (version-gap need, capability advertisement) — not yet in code; curriculum currently shows only the subscription input

Resolve CellRole Support discrepancy: merged ground-truth peat.md omits Support, but direct read of peat-protocol/src/models/role.rs:25 confirms it exists (7 variants). Per decision D1 source wins and Support was retained; reconcile the ground-truth doc to match
File/link a tracking issue for leader-election score-over-message convergence (leader_election.rs:340-345 follows higher node id with an in-code 'future work' comment; no issue located)

Confirm operator-guide config against compiled code: [security.pki] X.509 device-cert flow, CoT bind_port 8087 / type strings / command_authority enum surface (currently labeled Documented, not Shipped)
Verify quickstart node-count/latency by an actual Module 8 build/test run (audit was read-only per decision D3)
Verify crates.io / Maven Central publication status of PEAT crates (registries not checked)
Confirm standalone-repo existence/status: peat-sim, peat-registry, peat-sdk-go, a standalone peat-android repo
unverifiable_claims: 2026-06-22 delta read-only: ADR-072 (synced-folder lifecycle) and ADR-059 (data-as-a-capability) are Proposed with no code. peat-ffi connect_peer_nowait (lib.rs:1222) + relay-n0-hosted facade forward (peat-protocol/Cargo.toml:123, peat-ffi/Cargo.toml:109) and peat-node v0.4.8 inbox layout-mirror + path re-sanitisation + post-write blob_size check (src/attachments/inbox.rs:79-123) read from source/CHANGELOG, not exercised by a live run

ADR-071 collection plumbing: publish writes collection:None (file_distribution.rs ~963 TODO), so the interest-driven convergence path is wired but never exercised end-to-end at this HEAD — the seam (NeedEvaluator, CollectionSubscriptionNeed, with_need_evaluator) is unit/single-process tested only; no live multi-node interest-driven delivery confirmed

Provider-gossip runtime behaviour (peat/blob-announce/1): DEFAULT_ANNOUNCE_TTL=3-hop epidemic convergence, re-announce-on-acquire, and relay/holepunch dial to a non-adjacent holder are read from source + e2e test names (3 real-iroh tests) but not independently benchmarked for actual multi-hop reach under partition
peat-node attachment outbox watcher: PEAT_NODE_ATTACHMENT_OUTBOX_WATCH env gate and poll-based stability/dedup read from src/attachments/outbox.rs; not exercised by a live run this read-only audit

Existence at the audited HEAD of a peat-quickstart binary; peat-mesh-node production-vs-demo status; PEAT_BIND_PORT 4040 / PEAT_HTTP_PORT 8080 defaults; PEAT_CONNECTION_RECYCLE_SECS env var and its 60s/5s behavior

Exact SyncMessageType discriminant byte values; connection-health RTT/loss thresholds; discovery 60s candidate-pool timeout; specific Prometheus metric names and /api/v1/... endpoints; stream-ID range enforcement at runtime

External-spec / design-target numbers (flagged, not asserted): BLE rate/range; LoRa/Iridium SBD figures; ~10 MB Automerge / 256 KB / ~40x memory comparison; <5s P1 latency; 1,000+ node validation (1023 sim ceiling, #724-#727); 20-node-plateau thesis; 500,000/4,000 vs 9,120/384 connection-model disagreement
Operator-guide config not traced into compiled code: [security.pki] X.509 device-cert flow; CoT bind_port 8087 / type strings; command_authority enum surface (labeled Documented)

crates.io / Maven Central publication of any PEAT crate; peat-sim, peat-registry, peat-sdk-go, standalone peat-android repo existence (only ADR/epic anchors corroborate: ADR-054, #724, Phase-3 roadmap)

Spec-defined, implementation unconfirmed: Automerge ActorId byte construction; access-level ladder Public(0)..Critical(4) + audit retention tiers (90d/1y/2y); partition original-leader-keeps-Cell-ID rule; leader-election score-over-message convergence; EdgeIntelligence variant / >=3-sensor WideAreaObservation threshold (dropped/softened); spec line count ~3,750 (dropped)
